advantages: Persistent visual UI — variables, call stack, watches all stay on screen.
Gutter breakpoints don't pollute source; no risk of committing them.
Conditional / hit-count / logpoint / exception breakpoints with no syntax to remember.
Attach mode debugs long-running and remote processes that pdb can't.
Multi-thread / async-task aware out of the box.
Shareable `launch.json` / Run Configurations standardise the team's debug workflow.
disadvantages: Requires IDE setup; not always available on remote / SSH-only environments.
Initial config (`launch.json`, `debugpy.listen`) takes time to get right.
Heavier than pdb — overkill for a one-line `breakpoint()` investigation.
Vendor-locked debug profiles can drift across team members on different IDEs.
`debugpy.wait_for_client()` accidentally left enabled blocks a non-interactive process.
